agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"InventorySyncService","to":"ExternalInventoryAPI","status":"risk","issue":"No timeout configured on outbound HTTP call. A hung connection will block the sync indefinitely, potentially causing overlapping syncs or resource exhaustion if the 5-minute cron fires again while a previous call is still waiting."},{"from":"InventorySyncService","to":"LocalDatabase","status":"risk","issue":"If the external API returns partial or malformed data due to a degraded upstream, there is no validation gate before writing to the local DB. Corrupt stock counts could propagate to downstream consumers (e.g., storefront showing items in stock that are actually sold out)."},{"from":"SchedulerCron","to":"InventorySyncService","status":"risk","issue":"No mutual-exclusion or leader-election mechanism described. If the service scales to multiple replicas, every replica will independently sync on the same 5-minute interval, causing duplicate writes and amplified load on the external API."}],"contract_compliance":"Unknown — no schema validation on the external API response. If the upstream changes its payload shape, the service will silently write bad data or throw unhandled exceptions.","data_flow_integrity":"At risk. The path from External API → local DB has no idempotency guard, no payload validation, and no checksum or version comparison. A full-replace sync with no diffing means transient API errors could zero out stock counts."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["Logs use console.log instead of a structured logger — no JSON output, no log levels, no correlation IDs. Impossible to filter or alert on in any log aggregation tool.","No logging of sync duration, number of records processed, or number of records changed — the on-call engineer cannot distinguish a healthy sync from one that processed zero records.","Error logging captures only err.message, discarding the stack trace, HTTP status code, and response body. At 3am this is the difference between a 5-minute fix and a 2-hour investigation.","No logging at sync-start. Without a start marker, you cannot detect a sync that hung mid-execution."]},"metrics":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No counter for sync_success_total / sync_failure_total — cannot alert on failure rate.","No histogram for sync_duration_seconds — cannot detect slow degradation.","No gauge for records_synced_count — cannot detect silent data-loss (e.g., API returns empty array).","No gauge for last_successful_sync_timestamp — the single most important metric for a periodic sync job. Without it, nobody knows if sync has been broken for hours."]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed trace context propagated to the external API call — if the inventory provider supports tracing, we lose end-to-end visibility.","No span for the database write phase — cannot isolate whether latency is on the API side or DB side."]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer seeing 'sync failed: connection reset' in unstructured logs has no context: which products were affected, how many retries occurred, what the response code was, or how long the call took. Debugging requires SSH-ing into the container and adding ad-hoc logging — unacceptable for production."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"External inventory API is down (5xx or network unreachable)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, base 2s, max 30s, jitter). Log each retry attempt with attempt number and wait duration."},{"scenario":"External API responds but is extremely slow (30s+ latency)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a 10-second timeout on the HTTP client. Abort and log on timeout. Prevents resource exhaustion and overlapping syncs."},{"scenario":"External API returns 200 but with empty or malformed payload","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate response schema before writing to DB. If record count drops by >90% versus last sync, treat as anomalous and alert rather than blindly writing zeros."},{"scenario":"Database write fails mid-sync (partial update)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Wrap the DB write in a transaction. Either all stock counts update or none do. Log the transaction outcome."},{"scenario":"Sync takes longer than 5 minutes, next cron fires","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a mutex/lock (in-process flag or distributed lock if multi-replica). Skip the new run if a sync is already in progress, and log a warning."},{"scenario":"Service runs as multiple replicas","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement leader election or use a distributed lock (e.g., DB advisory lock, Redis lock) so only one replica syncs at a time."}],"retry_config":"None. Single attempt, silent failure. Must add retries with exponential backoff and jitter.","timeout_config":"None. The HTTP call can hang indefinitely. Must add a 10s connect timeout and 15s read timeout at minimum.","circuit_breakers":"None. If the external API is degraded for hours, every 5-minute sync hammers it without backing off. Add a circuit breaker that opens after 5 consecutive failures and half-opens after 2 minutes.","graceful_degradation":"None. When sync fails, downstream consumers have no way to know the data is stale. Expose a last_successful_sync_at timestamp that downstream services or dashboards can check."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Re-deploy previous container image. No database migrations in this release, so rollback is clean. Verify post-rollback that the previous sync schedule resumes correctly.","monitoring_coverage":"Nonexistent. No metrics, no health endpoint, no alerting. The team will not know this service is broken until a customer reports incorrect stock levels — potentially hours or days later.","on_call_documentation":"Missing. Requires a runbook covering: (1) how to verify sync is healthy, (2) how to manually trigger a sync, (3) how to check last successful sync time, (4) what to do when the external API is down, (5) how to verify DB stock counts are current.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["INVENTORY_API_TIMEOUT_MS — configurable HTTP timeout","SYNC_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS — max retry count","SYNC_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_MS — base delay for exponential backoff"],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add /health endpoint route returning sync status and last_successful_sync_at","Configure structured logging output (JSON format) for log aggregation compatibility"],"infra_provisioning":["Metrics endpoint (Prometheus /metrics or equivalent) for scraping sync_duration, sync_success_total, sync_failure_total, last_successful_sync_timestamp gauges","Alerting rules: alert if last_successful_sync_timestamp is older than 15 minutes, alert if sync_failure_total rate exceeds 3 consecutive failures"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema migrations in this release. Stock count updates are DML only. Rollback is safe."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Zero metrics instrumentation and no health endpoint","reason":"Without a /health endpoint, the orchestrator (k8s/ECS) cannot detect an unhealthy instance. Without metrics, there is no way to alert on sync failures or staleness. The team will learn about outages from customer complaints, not dashboards. This is the highest-priority blocker.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No timeout, no retries, no circuit breaker on external API dependency","reason":"A single hung HTTP call can block the sync loop indefinitely. Repeated failures hammer the external API with no backoff. These are not nice-to-haves — they are table-stakes for any service calling an external dependency. Shipping without them means a single upstream hiccup can cascade into stale inventory data for hours with no alert and no recovery.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"No protection against overlapping syncs or multi-replica duplication","reason":"If sync takes >5 minutes or the service runs multiple replicas, concurrent syncs will race on DB writes. This can cause stock count flickering or duplicate API load. Need a mutex or leader-election mechanism before production.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Resilience gaps are clear and well-understood patterns","Observability gaps are objective — zero metrics, zero health checks","Rollback safety is straightforward with no migrations"],"low_confidence_areas":["Cannot verify exact HTTP client library in use, so timeout/retry recommendations are generic","Cannot confirm whether the scheduler prevents overlapping executions natively","Cannot assess database write pattern (upsert vs. delete-and-replace) without seeing code"],"assumptions":["Service runs on a container orchestrator that relies on /health for liveness checks","External inventory API is a standard REST/HTTP endpoint","The 5-minute schedule is a simple cron/interval timer with no built-in overlap protection"]}}
